methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
IQM Quantum Computers is a pre-IPO quantum computing company that has raised €600M+. The relevant comp universe comprises 11 peers across the quantum computing sector: three publicly traded companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Systems) and eight private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this valuation. The quantum computing sector contains only seven same-sector peers overall, but none meet minimum sample-size thresholds when filtered by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier. Specifically, zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-tier comps exist in the filtered dataset. This shortfall—requiring a minimum of three datapoints per method—precludes reliable peer-median triangulation. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of business models and development stages within quantum computing.

Without triangulated methods supported by sufficient comparable data, no valuation range can be defensibly derived from this analysis. The key uncertainty is the extreme scarcity of pre-IPO quantum computing companies at equivalent maturity and fundraising scale. Prospective valuation work on IQM would require either (1) alternative methodologies (DCF, precedent transaction) with proprietary assumptions, or (2) expansion of the comp set to adjacent deep-tech hardware sectors, both of which fall outside the scope of peer-based triangulation.
